title: Grassy Canadian Quinoa Pilaf
description: A comforting and healthy dish featuring quinoa, vegetables, and a hint of grassy flavor. Perfect for a weeknight dinner or lunch.
instructions: Rinse the quinoa and cook according to package instructions with 2 cups of water.
In a separate pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat and sauté the chopped green onions and minced garlic until softened.
Add the chopped baby kale and diced zucchini to the pan and cook until the kale is wilted.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked quinoa, sautéed vegetables, lemon juice, dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
Transfer the mixture to a parchment-lined baking dish and cover with a sheet of parchment paper to create a papillote.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes or until the quinoa is tender and the flavors are combined.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley before serving.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Refrigerate any leftovers for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
When reheating, add a splash of water or broth to restore the quinoa's texture.
You can also customize this recipe by adding your favorite vegetables or herbs to suit your taste.
This dish is a great option for a weeknight dinner or lunch, as it's healthy, easy to make, and can be customized to suit your dietary needs.
It's also a great way to get in some greens and whole grains!
